policies.name: Universal Pre-K (Ages 3-4)
policies.description: Federally funded universal pre-K. Perry Preschool RCT shows $7-12 ROI per dollar over 40 years.
policies.rationale: Perry Preschool RCT: 40-year follow-up shows $7-12 ROI per dollar. Participants had 15% higher income, 20% less likely to be arrested, healthier outcomes. France, Denmark, Finland all have universal pre-K with better PISA scores.
policies.currentStatus: Only 34% of US 3-year-olds enrolled; varies wildly by state
policies.recommendedTarget: Federal funding for universal enrollment by age 3

policies.name: Pragmatic Clinical Trial Funding Reform
policies.description: Redirect NIH funding to pragmatic real-world trials. UK NIHR produces actionable evidence at 1/10th the cost.
policies.rationale: NIH spends $48B/yr but 70%+ goes to indirect costs. 85% of findings fail to replicate. UK NIHR model: pragmatic trials embedded in NHS produce actionable evidence at 1/10th the cost. PCORI pragmatic trials show 3x faster clinical adoption.
policies.currentStatus: NIH: $48B/yr, <10% on pragmatic trials, 85% of findings fail to replicate
policies.recommendedTarget: Mandate 30%+ of NIH budget for pragmatic trials with open data requirements

policies.name: Singapore-Style Healthcare (Universal + Competition)
policies.description: Mandatory health savings (Medisave) + catastrophic insurance (MediShield) + price transparency + hospital competition. South Korea achieves LE 83.6 at $3,588/cap; US gets LE 76.9 at $10,333/cap.
policies.rationale: OECD cheapest-high-performer analysis: South Korea achieves LE 83.6 at $3,588/cap. Japan achieves LE 84.5 at $4,095/cap. US achieves LE 76.9 at $10,333/cap (2.9x overspend). Singapore model: mandatory savings + catastrophic coverage + market competition achieves LE 83.9 at $4,300/cap. Savings: $2.3T/yr.
policies.currentStatus: US spends $10,333/cap, ranks 28/28 among panel countries. LE 76.9.
policies.recommendedTarget: South Korea model ($3,588/cap floor). $2.3T/yr savings → Universal Dividend.

policies.name: Military Spending Reduction to High-Performer Floor
policies.description: Reduce military spending to the level of the cheapest country with top-quartile life expectancy. Switzerland achieves LE 83.4 at $389/cap; US gets LE 76.9 at $2,052/cap.
policies.rationale: OECD cheapest-high-performer analysis: Switzerland achieves LE 83.4 at $389/cap military spending. Japan achieves LE 84.5 at $400/cap. US achieves LE 76.9 at $2,052/cap (5.3x overspend). Military spending has R²=0.012 correlation with life expectancy — essentially zero. $564B/yr savings → $4,241/household/yr as Universal Dividend.
policies.currentStatus: US spends $2,052/cap, ranks 27/28. 5.3x overspend vs cheapest high performer.
policies.recommendedTarget: Phased reduction to $389/cap (Switzerland floor). $564B/yr savings → Universal Dividend.

policies.name: Earned Income Tax Credit Expansion
policies.description: Expand EITC for childless workers. Directly increases after-tax income for low-income workers.
policies.rationale: EITC lifts ~6M people out of poverty annually. Research shows improved infant health, better school performance, and increased labor force participation. Effect on median income is modest (~1-3%) since it targets below-median workers.
policies.currentStatus: $63B annual cost; benefits phase out at $59K for families
policies.recommendedTarget: Double benefit for childless workers ($1,500→$3,000); raise income cap

policies.name: Shift Drug Policy from Criminal to Health Approach
policies.description: Decriminalize personal drug use, redirect enforcement budget to treatment. Based on Portugal (2001).
policies.rationale: Portugal decriminalized in 2001: drug deaths dropped 80%, HIV among users dropped 90%, treatment uptake tripled. US spends $40B/yr on drug enforcement with zero measurable reduction in drug deaths (r=0.026). Czech Republic, Switzerland, Netherlands show similar results.
policies.currentStatus: US spends $40B/yr on enforcement; 1.5M arrests/yr; overdose deaths at record highs
policies.recommendedTarget: Decriminalize personal use, redirect $40B/yr enforcement budget to treatment

policies.name: Federal Incentives for Zoning Reform
policies.description: Federal grants conditioned on local upzoning. Tokyo, Minneapolis, Oregon show reduced housing cost growth.
policies.rationale: Tokyo has no housing crisis because they allow building. Minneapolis eliminated single-family zoning: rents stabilized. Oregon statewide upzoning reduced housing cost growth. Hsieh & Moretti (2019) estimate $1.6T/yr GDP cost from restrictive zoning, though median income effect is smaller.
policies.currentStatus: 75% of US residential land zoned single-family only
policies.recommendedTarget: Condition federal transportation/HUD grants on local zoning reform

policies.name: Permanent Expanded Child Tax Credit
policies.description: Make the 2021 expanded CTC permanent and fully refundable. Cut child poverty 46% in 6 months.
policies.rationale: 2021 expanded CTC cut child poverty by 46% in 6 months — largest single-year reduction ever. Canada's similar program reduced child poverty by 30%. Improved childhood nutrition and health outcomes. Cost: ~$100B/yr.
policies.currentStatus: Reverted to $2,000/child, not fully refundable (excludes poorest families)

note: Generated using Bradford Hill scoring and welfare calculation from real cross-country evidence.
